questions.q: By what popular nickname is A. P. J. Abdul Kalam best known in India?
options: The Father of the Indian Constitution
The Father of the Indian Nuclear Programme
questions.explanation: Kalam earned the title 'Missile Man of India' for his leadership of the country's missile development programmes.

questions.q: Which high constitutional office did A. P. J. Abdul Kalam hold from 2002 to 2007?
questions.explanation: Kalam served as the 11th President of India from 2002 to 2007.

questions.q: What was A. P. J. Abdul Kalam's profession before he entered public life as President?
questions.explanation: Kalam trained as an aerospace engineer and spent decades as a scientist in India's space and defence programmes.

questions.q: In which Indian state was A. P. J. Abdul Kalam born?
questions.explanation: Kalam was born in Rameswaram, in the southern state of Tamil Nadu, in 1931.

questions.q: Kalam is closely associated with which Indian space launch vehicle, India's first successful satellite launcher?
questions.explanation: Kalam was the project director for the SLV-III, which in 1980 made India able to launch its own satellite into orbit.

questions.q: What is the highest civilian award of India, which Kalam received in 1997?
questions.explanation: Kalam was conferred the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour, in 1997.

questions.q: Kalam was widely admired for his connection with which group of people?
questions.explanation: Kalam was a beloved teacher who devoted much of his life to inspiring students and young people across India.

questions.q: Two Indian organisations Kalam worked closely with were ISRO and which defence research agency?
questions.explanation: Kalam led major projects at the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), as well as at ISRO.

questions.q: In which year did A. P. J. Abdul Kalam pass away?
questions.explanation: Kalam died on 27 July 2015 after collapsing while delivering a lecture in Shillong.

questions.q: Which of these is a famous autobiographical book written by A. P. J. Abdul Kalam?
questions.explanation: 'Wings of Fire' is Kalam's well-known autobiography, co-written with Arun Tiwari.

questions.q: What does the full name 'A. P. J.' in A. P. J. Abdul Kalam stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Avul Pakir Jainulabdeen Abdul Kalam, often abbreviated to A. P. J. Abdul Kalam.

questions.q: Kalam was the chief architect of which integrated programme that developed missiles like Agni and Prithvi?
questions.explanation: Kalam led the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP), which produced missiles such as Agni and Prithvi.

questions.q: Kalam played a key organisational role in which 1998 series of nuclear tests conducted by India?
questions.explanation: Kalam was a central figure in the 1998 Pokhran-II nuclear tests, which India also called Operation Shakti.

questions.q: Before becoming President, what scientific advisory role to the Government of India did Kalam hold?
options: Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government
questions.explanation: Kalam served as Principal Scientific Adviser to the Government of India, with cabinet rank, before his presidency.

questions.q: Kalam's SLV-III placed which satellite into orbit in July 1980?
questions.explanation: The SLV-III successfully placed the Rohini (RS-1) satellite into orbit in July 1980 with Kalam as project director.

questions.q: Kalam co-authored a visionary book outlining a roadmap for India to become a developed nation. What is it called?
options: India 2020: A Vision for the New Millennium
questions.explanation: Kalam co-wrote 'India 2020: A Vision for the New Millennium', laying out a roadmap for national development.

questions.q: Kalam earned his degree in aeronautical engineering from which institution in Chennai?
questions.explanation: Kalam studied aeronautical engineering at the Madras Institute of Technology (MIT) in Chennai.

questions.q: The Agni missile, developed under Kalam's leadership, belongs to which class of missiles?
questions.explanation: Agni is a ballistic missile, while Prithvi (also from the IGMDP) is a short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile.

questions.q: Kalam is often described as the 'People's President' because of which trait of his term?
options: He refused to live in the presidential palace
His accessibility and popularity, especially with ordinary citizens and youth
He had no prior public service
questions.explanation: Kalam was nicknamed the 'People's President' for his accessibility and his enormous popularity with citizens and youth.

questions.q: In which city did Kalam collapse and die in 2015 while delivering a lecture to students?
questions.explanation: Kalam suffered a cardiac arrest and died while lecturing at the Indian Institute of Management Shillong in 2015.

questions.q: Kalam contributed to a low-cost coronary stent and a lightweight caliper. These showcase his interest in applying defence technology to which field?
questions.explanation: Kalam promoted spin-off uses of aerospace technology in healthcare, including a low-cost coronary stent and a lightweight calliper.

questions.q: What was the approximate year Kalam joined India's space research effort, working under leaders such as Vikram Sarabhai?
questions.explanation: Kalam joined India's nascent space programme in the 1960s, working under pioneers including Vikram Sarabhai.

questions.q: Early in his career at the DRDO's predecessor, Kalam led the design of a small hovercraft. What was it named?
questions.explanation: One of Kalam's earliest projects was designing a small indigenous hovercraft named Nandi.

questions.q: Kalam's exact year and place of birth are:
questions.explanation: Kalam was born on 15 October 1931 in the temple town of Rameswaram, Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Kalam's birthday, 15 October, is observed in India as which themed day?
questions.explanation: In India, Kalam's birthday, 15 October, is observed as World Students' Day in recognition of his lifelong work with students and youth.

questions.q: Which DRDO surface-to-air missile system developed under the IGMDP shares its name with the Sanskrit word for 'sky'?
questions.explanation: Akash, meaning 'sky', was one of the missiles developed under the IGMDP that Kalam directed.

questions.q: Kalam belonged to which Indian political affiliation when elected President in 2002?
options: He was the Indian National Congress nominee
He was an independent backed across party lines
He was a Bharatiya Janata Party member of parliament
He was a member of the Communist Party
questions.explanation: Kalam was a non-partisan scientist who won the presidency with broad cross-party support rather than as a party politician.

questions.q: After his presidency, Kalam became a visiting professor and was associated with launching which youth-focused mission?
options: The 'What Can I Give' movement to fight corruption
questions.explanation: In his later years Kalam championed the 'What Can I Give Movement', encouraging youth to fight corruption through individual contribution.

questions.q: Wheeler Island off the Odisha coast, a key missile-testing site, was renamed in Kalam's honour. What is its current name?
questions.explanation: In 2015 the missile-testing site Wheeler Island, off Odisha, was renamed Abdul Kalam Island in his memory.

questions.q: Besides the Bharat Ratna, Kalam received the Padma Vibhushan and which other top civilian award earlier in his career?
questions.explanation: Kalam was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 1981 and the Padma Vibhushan in 1990, before receiving the Bharat Ratna in 1997.
